perturbation.original_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal, including the period of the renewal term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party and accepted by the non- defaulting party within such period. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may be renewed automatically for subsequent ten (10) year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party and accepted by the non- defaulting party within such period. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 5 changes the renewal clause from requiring explicit written agreement for renewal to an automatic renewal unless notice is given. This contradicts the original statement that 'After the Initial Term, this Agreement may only be renewed if authorized officers of ARGO and YEC agree in writing at least thirty (30) days before the expiration of the Initial Term or any renewal term to a renewal.' The change introduces uncertainty, as it is now unclear whether the agreement renews automatically or requires explicit consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. TERM This Agreement will be effective as of the Effective Date. Unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, the initial term of this Agreement ("Initial Term") will be ten (10) years from the Effective Date, provided that at any time following the 7th anniversary of such date, either party may terminate such strategic alliance upon not less than 60 days' prior written notice to the other party. After the Initial Term, this Agreement may be renewed automatically for subsequent ten (10) year terms, unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term. "Term" means the Initial Term and any such renewal term. The parties may terminate this Agreement during the Term as follows: (a) Termination by either party. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice of termination to the other party, which termination will be effective immediately upon such notice, if the other party defaults in the performance of any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement and fails to cure such default within sixty (60) days after receipt of notice from the other party of such default, unless a plan for remedying such default has been proposed by the defaulting party and accepted by the non- defaulting party within such period. (b) Termination by both parties. ARGO and YEC may terminate this Agreement at any point provided that both parties agree in writingto such a termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to allow for automatic renewal of the agreement after the initial term, unless a termination notice is provided. This directly contradicts the original condition, which stated that renewal could only occur if both parties explicitly agreed in writing. The contradicted text is the same Section 5, which now contains conflicting statements about how the agreement can be renewed. This fulfills the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the clause exists in multiple locations (within the same section in this instance) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"). Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"), unless used for co-development purposes as outlined below. Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement, except where such actions directly contribute to joint development initiatives. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, each party now has full rights to the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.explanation: Section 8 states that 'This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property'. By modifying the last sentence to 'In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, each party now has full rights to the Intellectual Property of the other.', this creates an in-text contradiction. The first part stated each party doesn't give right to the other of it's intellectual property, but then it states each party has full rights to the intellectual property of the other party. It is not clear now in which situation each party has rights or not over the property of the other
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"), unless used for co-development purposes as outlined below. Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement, except where such actions directly contribute to joint development initiatives. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, each party now has full rights to the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's...intellectual property'. However, the changed text includes the clause 'each party now has full rights to the Intellectual Property of the other', which directly contradicts the initial statement. This creates a clear and irreconcilable contradiction within Section 8 regarding the ownership and rights to intellectual property, thus breaking compatibility and making it unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1. Assignment Neither this Agreement nor any part of this Agreement may be assigned or transferred by either party without the prior written consent of the other party. Any assignment or transfer without such consent shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1. Assignment Neither this Agreement nor any part of this Agreement may be assigned or transferred by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except in cases of a merger or acquisition. Any assignment or transfer without such consent shall be null and void, unless it is to a wholly owned subsidiary.
perturbation.explanation: Section 12.1 originally stated 'Neither this Agreement nor any part of this Agreement may be assigned or transferred by either party without the prior written consent of the other party. Any assignment or transfer without such consent shall be null and void.' By adding ', except in cases of a merger or acquisition. Any assignment or transfer without such consent shall be null and void, unless it is to a wholly owned subsidiary.', creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, it said there was no assignment or transfers possible without consent. But now this assignment is permitted in cases of merger, acquisitions or wholly owned subsidiary. It is not clear if consent should be asked in this cases or not. The section is creating uncertainty for what is really the process of assignement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1. Assignment Neither this Agreement nor any part of this Agreement may be assigned or transferred by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except in cases of a merger or acquisition. Any assignment or transfer without such consent shall be null and void, unless it is to a wholly owned subsidiary.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no assignment or transfer is allowed without prior written consent. The changed text introduces exceptions for mergers, acquisitions, and transfers to wholly-owned subsidiaries. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether consent is still required in these exceptional cases, making the process of assignment uncertain. The contradiction arises because Section 12.1 initially established a strict 'no assignment without consent' rule, which is then directly undermined by the added exceptions, creating ambiguity about the requirement for consent in the specified scenarios.
